title: Live Ninja — User Settings (v1)
description: Canonical per-user settings document. Stored as DynamoDB item PK=USER#<userId> SK=SETTINGS#v<n> (see PRD.md §8.1). This is the ONLY settings document across all three surfaces (web, Android, M5Stack) — FR-S01. Every reader/writer, including firmware written years apart, MUST preserve unknown fields on write-back per contracts/README.md rule 2. additionalProperties is true at every object level for exactly this reason.

version.description: Optimistic-concurrency counter (FR-S01). Every PUT/UpdateItem carries ConditionExpression version = expected and increments this on success; a stale write gets HTTP 409 and the client re-reads/re-applies. This is NOT a schema version — schema evolution is additive-only per contracts/README.md and is not tracked by this field.

wakeWord.description: ID of the active wake-word catalog entry (FR-K02). Always a combobox selection over the shared catalog + the user's own trained models — never a free-typed phrase. Resolves via GET /v1/wakeword catalog and the per-user WAKE# items; the actual model asset is fetched via contracts/wakeword-manifest.md.

wakeEngine.description: Wake-word inference engine backing the selected wakeWord (FR-K02, FR-M02). `openwakeword` is the free/default engine (Android FGS, web WASM). `porcupine` is the optional Picovoice engine (Android; requires an AccessKey — reveals that field via progressive disclosure, UX-R05). `wakenet` is the M5Stack ESP-SR primary on-device engine (FR-M02); M5Stack falls back to an oWW-ESP model when a custom phrase isn't available as a curated WakeNet model (FR-K05).

sensitivity.description: Wake-word detection sensitivity, always a bounded slider — never a text field (FR-K06).

persona.description: Persona selection (FR-S03). Sent/stored as a catalog ID; the server resolves the actual system instructions server-side (anti prompt-injection — clients never send raw instructions to the realtime broker). `presetId: "custom"` is the sole justified free-text case (UX rich-UI case 6): it reveals `systemInstructions` as a capped, labeled editor with reset-to-preset.

presetId.description: Catalog persona ID (e.g. "default") or the literal "custom".

systemInstructions.description: Free-text system-instruction override. Only meaningful/editable when presetId == "custom"; null/absent otherwise. Capped length enforced server-side (see api.md PUT /v1/settings validation).

voice.description: OpenAI Realtime voice, chosen from an enumerated picker with inline preview (UX-R01) — never free text. Default is `cedar` per PRD Q-14. New voice enum members may be appended in later milestones per contracts/README.md rule 3; an unrecognized value on read must fall back to `cedar` in the UI but be preserved verbatim on write-back.

turnDetection.description: Turn-detection strategy bound into the realtime session config at mint (FR-V01, PRD §6.2). Exposed to the user as a radio control (UX-R01/R02). `semantic_vad` is the platform default and pairs with `interrupt_response:true`.

micDeviceId.description: Selected input device id from the browser's enumerateDevices()/Android getAvailableCommunicationDevices() (UX-R01) — populated control, never typed. Null means "system default device".

voiceEngine.description: Voice-engine selection (FR-VE-01..04). `default` applies to any session/device without an explicit pin (e.g. a fresh web browser session); `devices` is a per-device pin map keyed by deviceId, letting an individual M5Stack/Android device be pinned to a cheaper or alternate engine (e.g. nova-sonic) while others stay on the platform default. The realtime-broker resolves engine-for-session as devices[deviceId] ?? default. `nova-sonic` reintroduces a backend media bridge for ONLY the pinned device (FR-VE-04) — all other engines stay client-direct.

devices.description: Map of deviceId -> pinned engine. Keys are the caller's own DEVICE#<deviceId> ids; absent keys fall back to `default`.

privacy.description: Retention/storage controls (FR-S04). Included here because Settings is the one canonical doc (FR-S01) and these fields gate DynamoDB TTL + S3 lifecycle enforcement; not in the required core list above because it predates no milestone dependency, but MUST be present on every settings document the server writes at account-creation time.

storeAudio.description: Persist raw audio to S3 (opt-in). Off by default — on-device wake + this flag are the load-bearing privacy controls (PRD §10).

retentionDays.description: TTL window for transcripts/logs. Radio control, not free text (FR-S04).

micEagerness.description: Mic pickup: how quickly semantic VAD treats a pause as end-of-turn (maps to the realtime API's turn_detection.eagerness). Optional; absent normalizes to auto.

appearance.description: Visual customization (two axes, podcasts.jeremy.ninja-style): a style preset and an accent color. Optional; absent normalizes to the defaults.

accentColor.description: #rrggbb, or empty string for the style's default accent.
